titleStmt.title: Letter from the Chief Secretary’s Office to Francis Caldwell, 3 August 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from The Chief Secretary's Office to Francis Caldwell (c.1860-1934), Head Constable, Liverpool City Police Force.The CSO is considering the payment of a war bonus to the constabulary in Ireland, and is inquiring as to the practice in Liverpool.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5620/13692

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
DUBLIN CASTLE 3rd August, 1916. Dear Sir, The Irish Government have lately been
dealing with the question of a War Bonus for
the Police Forces in Ireland. In this conâ
nection it would be of interest to know what
Bonus, if any, has been granted to your Force
since the outbreak of the War, whether it
applies to all ranks, and to married and single
men. I should be much obliged if you could
supply this information. Yours faithfully, The Head Constable, City Police Force, Liverpool.
